title: QAI Ventures Launches Inaugural Singapore Quantum Accelerator Cohort to Anchor APAC Expansion - Quantum Computing Report
gist: QAI Ventures launched Singapore's first dedicated corporate quantum accelerator with Enterprise Singapore, selecting 4 startups from 63 applicants across 12 countries, offering SGD 300K each plus hardware sandbox access via IonQ, QuEra and Fujitsu to anchor APAC expansion.

insights.claim: QAI Ventures selected only 4 startups from 63 applications across 12 countries for its Singapore cohort, a ~6% acceptance rate signaling tight curation of the APAC quantum pipeline.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Low acceptance and 12-country reach show deep global deal flow funneling through Singapore as an APAC gateway.
insights.quote: selected four highly specialized international startups from a baseline of 63 global applications spanning 12 countries
insights.locator: The execution group selected four highly specialized

insights.claim: Each participant receives an SGD 300,000 early-stage package plus 12-month Singapore workspace and cloud hardware access to IonQ, QuEra, and Fujitsu QPUs, addressing the long lab-to-commercial timeline in deep tech.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: IonQ/QuEra/Fujitsu supplying the hardware sandbox positions them as default platforms for the next APAC quantum cohort.
insights.quote: SGD 300,000 baseline investment package per selected startup
insights.locator: To stabilize these long-range development tracks

insights.claim: The accelerator is structured to align with Singapore's National Quantum Strategy and runs in direct cooperation with Enterprise Singapore, showing state industrial policy actively co-funding quantum startup capture.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Singapore is deploying sovereign backing to become the APAC quantum hub—competitive pressure on regional rivals.
insights.quote: Aligned with the National Quantum Strategy
insights.locator: Operated in direct cooperation with Enterprise Singapore

insights.claim: The inaugural cohort spans diverse geographies and applications—Quantum Logic (Netherlands, cryo control hardware), Qualia Therapeutics (Armenia, neurostimulation), QPICs (US, photonic chips), and Regenesis Materials (Indonesia, sustainable materials).
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Early names to watch across cryo hardware, photonics, and quantum-enabled materials/health.
insights.locator: The selected inaugural cohort consists of four cross-disciplinary
